text:
Pisac (ピサク, Pisaku) Hunter × Hunter Hunter Association Official Issue: Hunter's Guide; Character & World Official Databook (pg. 207) was a Greed Island player. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 132 

Appearance

Pisac was a spindly dark-skinned man with a triangular-shaped head, slightly elongated ears, and his hair was shaven on the sides, while the top of his head had a perm. He wore a simple long-sleeved shirt, plain pants, and shoes.

Plot

Greed Island arc

Sometime after entering the game, Pisac is among a group of new players who are convinced to join Nickes' Alliance, presumably by Contarch. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 131 The new recruits wait in front of the Shiso Tree for Jispa's arrival. The moment Jispa arrives, he instantly uses an "Accompany" to transport them to Kosofftro, who sits somewhere near the alliance base. As the new recruits are led to the base by Jispa, he divulges to them that their first task is to collect certain spell cards in Masadora for the alliance. At the base, the new recruits are instructed to memorize a list of spell cards within an hour. 

When the alliance is close to collecting all of the cards in the game, Genthru reveals himself to be the Bomber and claims that he has placed bombs on every member of the alliance. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 142 Soon after this revelation and disclosing how to disarm the bombs, Genthru escapes using a "Leave" card. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 143 Eventually, the alliance members surrender all of their specified slot cards to the Bombers, hoping that they will disarm the bombs attached to them. However, the Bombers instead simultaneously detonate the bombs and kill nearly all of the alliance members. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 144 

Pisac's name is listed in Gon's binder. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 

Abilities & Powers

Since most of the alliance members joined because they lacked combat abilities, it can be assumed that Pisac also had none. 

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Pisac was capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Though Pisac's name is technically given in the manga through Gon's Greed Island binder, the correspondence between the character and his name is only provided in the Hunter's Guide databook. 
Pisac's skin and hair are mistakenly colored white and blond, respectively, in Episode 62 of the 2011 anime adaptation. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 62 (2011)
